title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Patrick suggests that Miss Maple may have been killed and theorizes that Mr. Quincannon is the murderer, believing she never left Chipping Cleaves. Letitia Blacklock defends Philip, arguing that Philip did not kill Miss Muggleton and noting that Muggleton died while Philip was at the police station. Letitia becomes distressed and screams about the fear of a killer, prompting Patrick to offer his protection, which Letitia receives with disappointment.

Micky announces she will not make dinner and retreats to her room, claiming she is frightened by shadows and noises in the house. Julia takes over dinner preparations, insisting that Patrick taste every dish to avoid accusations of poisoning. Following dinner, the group waits in the living room. Inspector Corrado calls to announce he is arriving with Colonel Cadorick and his wife, the Swetnams, and Mrs. Sweetnam.

In the kitchen, Julia argues with Midge over the use of cooking pans before heading to the front door to admit Miss Quincannon (also referred to as Miss Chicliff), who arrives looking relieved. The Cadoricks and Swetnams arrive, creating a grave atmosphere. Constable Colquhoun begins an investigation, stating that the police believe the murderer of Miss Mulgrave is a woman. He asks the women to provide their whereabouts between 4:00 PM and 4:20 PM. Julia, appearing pale but calm, states that from 4:00 PM to 4:30 PM, she was walking along a field by a stream leading to the farmhouse at Compton and did not encounter anyone or approach the Gravel Pit House.
